raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that finds .java files containing 'Hello' and prints names. Use grep -rl 'Hello' --include='*.java' . or find . -name '*.java' -exec grep -q 'Hello' {} \; -print. Simpler: grep -rl --include='*.java' 'Hello' .
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command that finds .java files containing 'Hello' and prints names. Use grep -rl 'Hello' --include='*.java' . or find . -name '*.java' -exec grep -q 'Hello' {} \; -print. Simpler: grep -rl --include='*.java' 'Hello' .

task.query: Find .java files in the testbed directory tree that contain 'Hello', and print their names
task.gold: find /testbed -name "*.java" -exec grep -Hin Hello {} + | cut -d ":" -f 1 | xargs -I{} basename {}

record.query: Find .java files in the testbed directory tree that contain 'Hello', and print their names
record.gold: find /testbed -name "*.java" -exec grep -Hin Hello {} + | cut -d ":" -f 1 | xargs -I{} basename {}
